description: Commander.js parent option stealing: when both the root program and a subcommand define the same option (e.g. --json), Commander recognizes the parent's option anywhere in argv by default, consuming the flag before the subcommand sees it. Subcommand opts.json is undefined even though the user passed --json after the subcommand name.
resolution: Call program.enablePositionalOptions() so parent options are only matched before the subcommand name. This lets ov --json status set the parent flag while ov status --json sets the subcommand flag.

content: Every ov CLI command must define --json as a local option on its Command instance. The parent program also defines --json (for help text and pre-subcommand usage), but enablePositionalOptions() ensures the parent does not steal the flag from subcommands. When adding a new command, always add .option('--json', 'Output as JSON') and handle it in the action handler.

name: headless-coordination-broken
description: Headless coordination is currently broken: agents spawned via 'ov sling' after 'ov serve' has started are not registered in the runtime connection registry, so neither mail-injection nor 'ov nudge' reaches them. Process is alive (ps confirms) but the agent is structurally deaf — never sees worker_done from its own builders, dispatch updates from coordinator, or sibling-lead status updates. Workaround for stuck headless leads: stop the lead, verify builder branches by reading worker_done mails (builders self-report gates and diff scope), merge directly. Symptoms: long-running lead with read=false on its inbox, 'ov nudge' returns 'No active session for agent <name>'. Filed overstory-41eb (P0). Confirmed in run 2026-04-29.

name: headless-stdin-fifo
description: Cross-process stdin delivery to headless agents uses a per-agent FIFO at .overstory/agents/<name>/stdin.fifo. ov sling opens it O_RDWR, passes the fd as the child's stdin, and exits — the FIFO file persists for any process to write to (mail injector in ov serve, ov nudge, ov mail). Writers use O_WRONLY|O_NONBLOCK so ENXIO returns 'no-reader' when the agent is gone. Replaced the module-level connection registry which only worked when sling and serve shared a process.

Module-level Maps for agent runtime state are invisible across CLI process boundaries. registerHeadlessConnection in src/runtimes/connections.ts (added by overstory-1f66/2a6d) populates an in-memory Map; but ov sling and ov serve run in separate processes, so sling's registration never reaches serve's mail-injection listener. Symptom: agents alive (ps confirms), inbox piling up for hours, ov nudge fails with 'No active session'.
resolution: Replace in-memory registry with a per-agent FIFO on disk at .overstory/agents/<name>/stdin.fifo. Any process can open and write to it. See src/agents/headless-stdin.ts and src/commands/serve.ts installMailInjectors.

description: Fresh worktrees from ov sling have no node_modules; quality gates require 'bun install' in both repo root and ui/. Additionally, 'bun run lint' (biome check .) silently matches zero files under biome 2.3.15 with the project's includes pattern; 'bunx biome check src' produces real lint output (261 files clean on main).
resolution: Run 'bun install' in worktree root and in ui/. For lint verification, prefer 'bunx biome check src' over 'bun run lint' until the package.json script is updated to use explicit globs instead of '.'.

description: killSession("") wildcard-killed entire tmux server when terminating spawn-per-turn agent — tmux -t is prefix matched, empty prefix matches all sessions; matrix in killAgent missed the (tmuxSession=="" && pid==null) arm. Defense in depth: tmux.killSession throws on empty name.
resolution: Branch killAgent() by session.tmuxSession === "" first: pid !== null kills the process tree, pid === null is a no-op (spawn-per-turn between turns); only the named-tmux path can reach tmux.killSession. Plus boundary guards in tmux.ts: killSession("") throws AgentError, isSessionAlive("") returns false without invoking tmux.

description: killSession('') wildcard-killed entire overstory tmux server when watchdog terminated spawn-per-turn agents. Root cause: tmux's '-t' is prefix-matched (unless prefixed with '='), so empty prefix matches every session. killAgent's matrix on (tmuxSession==='', pid!==null) had only patched (true,true); the (true,false) spawn-per-turn arm fell through to tmux.killSession(''). Lesson: when guarding a 4-arm matrix, enumerate all 4 — and treat unsafe primitives like tmux.killSession('') as boundary errors, not silent passes.
resolution: killAgent now branches on session.tmuxSession === '' first: pid !== null → proc.killTree, pid === null → no-op. tmux.killSession('') and isSessionAlive('') guard the empty-name boundary in src/worktree/tmux.ts. Regression tests cover spawn-per-turn no-op, long-lived headless pid kill, and the two boundary throws.

name: validate-worktree-after-add
description: validateWorktreeCreation post-creation check (overstory-6878): in src/worktree/manager.ts, after git worktree add, validate (1) listWorktrees(repoRoot) contains the new path AND (2) git ls-files inside the worktree returns non-empty. On failure, rollbackWorktree() then throw WorktreeError with worktreePath/branchName metadata. Catches the witnessed phantom-worktree mode (registered-but-empty + unregistered-but-dir-exists). End-to-end test fabricates a base branch pointing at an empty tree (git hash-object -t tree /dev/null + git commit-tree) to provoke registered-but-empty.
